opportunities.title: PDF receipt scanning support
opportunities.description: All major competitors (Dext, Expensify, Veryfi, Receipt-Wrangler) support PDF receipt uploads alongside image files. The app currently only handles image uploads. PDF receipts are increasingly common as digital invoices arrive via email. Adding PDF-to-image conversion or direct PDF text extraction would close a significant feature gap.
opportunities.evidence: https://github.com/ChamikaCSA/receipt-tracker supports PDF via Claude/GPT-4; Dext and Expensify handle PDFs natively; https://receiptmake.com/blog/best-receipt-scanning-apps

opportunities.title: Batch/multi-receipt upload and processing
opportunities.description: Competitors like ScanRelief and Dext allow users to upload multiple receipts at once and process them in parallel. The app currently handles single-receipt uploads. Batch processing would dramatically reduce friction for users catching up on expense entry.
opportunities.evidence: https://www.rho.co/blog/best-receipt-scanner-app; ScanRelief batch processing; Dext multi-source capture

opportunities.title: Export to CSV/PDF for accounting handoff
opportunities.description: Expensify, Smart Receipts, and QuickBooks all offer export functionality (CSV, PDF reports) for handing off expense data to accountants or importing into bookkeeping tools. The app has no export capability. For a German SKR03-focused app, DATEV-compatible CSV export would be particularly valuable.
opportunities.evidence: https://thecfoclub.com/tools/best-receipt-scanner-app/; Smart Receipts generates CSV/PDF reports; QuickBooks receipt-to-transaction export

opportunities.title: Fraud detection and duplicate receipt flagging
opportunities.description: Veryfi uses ML to flag unusual receipt patterns and inconsistencies. At minimum, detecting duplicate receipts (same vendor, amount, date) would prevent double-entry errors. This is a differentiator that most smaller tools lack.
opportunities.evidence: https://www.volopay.com/blog/best-receipt-scanning-apps/ - Veryfi fraud detection; spending pattern analysis

opportunities.title: Multi-currency support with automatic conversion
opportunities.description: The app targets German accounting (SKR03) but has no multi-currency handling. Users traveling internationally need receipts in EUR, USD, GBP etc. to be recognized and optionally converted. Zoho Expense and Expensify both handle this natively.
opportunities.evidence: Product uses German SKR03 categories suggesting business users who likely travel; https://www.klippa.com/en/blog/information/receipt-scanning-software/ - multi-currency as standard feature

opportunities.title: Receipt image preview in dashboard table rows
opportunities.description: Users currently upload receipts but the dashboard only shows extracted text data. Adding a thumbnail preview or lightbox for the original receipt image directly from table rows would let users verify OCR accuracy without leaving the dashboard. The file proxy routes already exist at /api/files/[fileId] and /api/files/[fileId]/thumbnail.
opportunities.evidence: Existing routes: src/app/api/files/[fileId]/route.ts, src/app/api/files/[fileId]/thumbnail/route.ts — infrastructure exists but is not surfaced in the UI

opportunities.title: Improved error handling and retry for failed OCR/classification
opportunities.description: The upload flow has no visible retry mechanism if OCR or AI classification fails. Users see no feedback on what went wrong. Adding error states, retry buttons, and partial-result handling would improve reliability perception.
opportunities.evidence: Code review: src/app/app/page.tsx upload flow, src/app/api/ocr/route.ts, src/app/api/classify-single/route.ts — no retry logic visible

opportunities.title: Replace Google Cloud Vision + OpenRouter with direct multimodal LLM for end-to-end extraction
opportunities.description: The current pipeline uses Google Cloud Vision for OCR then OpenRouter for AI classification — two API calls and a lossy text-extraction step. In 2026, multimodal LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini 2.5 Pro) can process receipt images directly and extract structured JSON in a single call with 95%+ accuracy, eliminating the OCR intermediary entirely. This reduces latency, cost, and complexity.
opportunities.evidence: https://photes.io/blog/posts/ocr-research-trend - multimodal LLMs replacing traditional OCR pipelines; GPT-5 at 95% on printed text; Azure Document Intelligence at 96%

opportunities.title: Add end-to-end tests for the upload-to-dashboard flow
opportunities.description: The project has zero test files. The upload -> OCR -> classify -> persist -> display pipeline is complex and touches multiple APIs. Adding integration tests (at minimum for the extraction logic and classification rules) would catch regressions and enable confident refactoring.
opportunities.evidence: No test files found in src/; no test script in package.json; extract-receipt-fields.ts and classification-rules.ts are pure functions ideal for unit testing

opportunities.title: Add PWA/mobile-responsive optimizations for phone-based receipt capture
opportunities.description: Receipt scanning is inherently mobile-first — users take photos on their phone. The app could add a service worker, manifest.json, and camera-capture input to enable install-to-homescreen and direct camera access on mobile devices, competing with native apps.
opportunities.evidence: https://getfinny.app/blog/best-receipt-scanner-apps-2026 - mobile-first is table stakes; Budget-Lens and SparkReceipt offer mobile-optimized capture
